scope: Network policy decision payloads and sandbox network proxy config-loader behavior

requiredBehaviors: parse ask and deny policy decisions with source and protocol aliases
return approval context only for ask decisions produced by the decider
reject approval context creation when protocol or non-blank host is absent
edgeCases: https_connect and http-connect both normalize to the HTTPS approval protocol
socks5_tcp and socks5_udp normalize to AgenC protocol strings
unknown decision, source, or protocol values throw instead of defaulting

requiredBehaviors: produce user-facing denial messages only for deny decisions
map known block reasons to explicit denial detail
map permission-layer network-policy amendments to exec-policy rule amendments
edgeCases: empty blocked host returns the generic blocked-by-policy message
permission-layer network_policy_amendment values are accepted without a second canonical amendment type
socks5 TCP and UDP amendments return exec-policy underscore protocol strings and justifications

requiredBehaviors: read raw default_permissions and permissions.<profile>.network TOML tables
apply network config layers in precedence order
expose a minimal reloadable config-state wrapper without starting a proxy listener
edgeCases: malformed sandbox network TOML throws instead of falling back to defaults
permission-profile network TOML ignores unsupported mitm fields instead of enabling proxy interception
permission-profile network domains reject unsupported none values at the TOML boundary
missing user config is the only silent no-layer default path
mtime reload updates config state after a config.toml write

requiredBehaviors: trusted layers are system, managed, and legacy_managed
user, project, and session_flags layers never contribute trusted constraints
trusted scalar constraints use last trusted layer wins
trusted domain constraints overlay by normalized pattern
edgeCases: trusted enabled=false rejects a user layer that enables the proxy
managed wildcard allow constraints only permit candidate allowlists covered by the managed pattern
global wildcard managed allow or deny constraints are rejected

requiredBehaviors: domain decisions check deny patterns before allow patterns
exact, star-dot, double-star-dot, and global wildcard patterns follow the reference matching model
exec-policy network rules are accepted as explicit loader input
edgeCases: exact deny blocks wildcard allow
wildcard deny blocks exact allow
malformed domain glob patterns fail closed during config-state construction
malformed bracketed explicit exec-policy hosts reject unsupported suffixes before host normalization
http allow plus socks5 deny projects to host denied in the domain-only config projection
prompt rules neither allow nor deny and do not erase existing host-protocol rules

approvedDivergence.reason: AgenC's current network proxy config projection is domain-only. To avoid widening a protocol-specific deny into an allow, projection keeps host+protocol rule identity internally and collapses any forbidden protocol for a host to a host-wide deny. Example: http allow plus socks5 deny yields host denied in domain-only config projection.
The sandbox-specific loader throws on malformed user network TOML instead of using the generic config loader warning fallback. Missing user config remains the only silent default path.
C-01g owns policy decision and config-loader behavior only. Starting HTTP/SOCKS listeners is outside this item.
